evidence_entries.evidence_text: Early in the movie dialogue, Pando is introduced as Crowbar Jones's sidekick: "I couldn't have done it without my trusty sidekick, Pando." Another line says, "Classic Pando."
evidence_entries.inference_hint: Establishes the character name and that the question is about Pando/Panda in the movie.

evidence_entries.evidence_text: The movie itself portrays Pando as comic relief. Dialogue around 56.95s–86.19s says, "Dude, everyone is laughing at Pando" and "He's the comedy relief."
evidence_entries.inference_hint: Grounds how the in-movie character is characterized in dialogue.

evidence_entries.evidence_text: Later movie dialogue depicts Pando as struggling: "Crowbar, help me!" "I think something's wrong with my jetpack!" and Crowbar responds, "Get it together, Pando."
evidence_entries.inference_hint: Supports traits like ineptness/clumsiness within the movie, but not any listed option.

evidence_entries.evidence_text: The movie also jokes about Pando's swimming and phone use: "If only I knew how to swim!" followed by "That's not true! I do know how to swim!" and later a viewer complains, "I don't spend all my time on the phone, I'm not clumsy, and I do know how to swim."
evidence_entries.inference_hint: Distinguishes the movie portrayal from a viewer's complaint that the portrayal is inaccurate.

evidence_entries.evidence_text: A viewer explicitly comments on the portrayal: "Pando is a huge misrepresentation of who I am." This confirms later complaints are about how the movie depicts Pando, not neutral narration of the real person.
evidence_entries.inference_hint: Important disambiguation between in-movie characterization and commentary on that characterization.

evidence.evidence_text: Early in the movie dialogue, Pando is introduced as Crowbar Jones's sidekick: "I couldn't have done it without my trusty sidekick, Pando." Another line says, "Classic Pando."
The movie itself portrays Pando as comic relief. Dialogue around 56.95s–86.19s says, "Dude, everyone is laughing at Pando" and "He's the comedy relief."
Later movie dialogue depicts Pando as struggling: "Crowbar, help me!" "I think something's wrong with my jetpack!" and Crowbar responds, "Get it together, Pando."

The movie also jokes about Pando's swimming and phone use: "If only I knew how to swim!" followed by "That's not true! I do know how to swim!" and later a viewer complains, "I don't spend all my time on the phone, I'm not clumsy, and I do know how to swim."

A viewer explicitly comments on the portrayal: "Pando is a huge misrepresentation of who I am." This confirms later complaints are about how the movie depicts Pando, not neutral narration of the real person.

transcript_segments.text: Congratulations, Crowbar Jones! You've done it again! Now the world is a safer place because of you, Chip Chip Cheerio. I'm just doing my job, sir. Well, a job well done deserves recognition and a medal of awesomeness. Also, your album went platinum while you were saving the world. Ah, stop it. This is too much. I couldn't have done it without my trusty sidekick, Pando. Oh, thank you, Mr. Crowbar. Huh? Huh? Ooh, not half bad.

Classic Pando. Oh, you guys are the best. It also makes me want to give you the keys to my Ferrari Turbo. Ah, Mr. President, we just couldn't. Wait a second. President would never give out his Ferrari Turbo to anyone. Who are you? Where's the real President? I knew it. A danger bot. What does this mean, Mr. Krollbar? It means that the President has been kidnapped.

transcript_segments.text: Are you guys good with popcorn and drinks? Do you need more pillows? Just holler at your boy if you need anything. Quick, man, can you make more popcorn? Looks like people are running low. Hey, what do you think? Dude, everyone is laughing at Pando. Cool, right? He's the comedy relief. Oh, look, there's a sale on rat traps. Meanwhile, back at the base. Who kidnapped the president? Could it have been Agent Cobra? Maybe it was my old nemesis, Neferio Dawson. It just doesn't make sense. Have you found anything, Pando?

Not really. Security camera didn't pick up anything unusual. Wait a second. Let me see the computer. There. Enhance. Evil robot headquarters? This goes deeper than I thought. Come on, Pando. Let's head to the lab. Right behind you, Mr. Jones!
Hello, Agent W. I'm stuck, Crowbar. Ah, Mr. Jones. Late as usual. Fashionably late, you mean? No, not really. All right, here you go.

transcript_segments.text: There's a cool explosion at the end. Wait, I swear this is a family film. I worked really hard on it. Okay, where's Grizz? He's gone too far this time.

Chris, I got beef with you, man. Pando is a huge misrepresentation of who I am. I don't spend all my time on the phone, I'm not clumsy, and I do know how to swim. It's okay, bro. I think I'm just gonna throw the movie in the trash anyways. Wait, really? The audience has spoken. Everyone hates my movie. All that hard work for nothing. It's all good, guys.
Oh, I mean, you don't have to throw it out. It's not that bad a film. Really? Yeah, in a childlike sort of way. Iceberg gives it five-ish stars.

preprocess.summary:
[00:00-00:02] Visual: A small, cartoonish house with a brown roof and a patchwork of different colored fabrics for walls is shown. It sits on a wooden surface, flanked by two small green bushes. In the background, a bookshelf is filled with books and a red thermos, and a large tree trunk is visible on the right. Two white thought bubbles float in the sky.
[00:00-00:26] Speech: Congratulations, Crowbar Jones! You've done it again! Now the world is a safer place because of you, Chip Chip Cheerio. I'm just doing my job, sir. Well, a job well done deserves recognition and a medal of awesomeness. Also, your album went platinum while you were saving the world. Ah, stop it. This is too much. I couldn't have done it without my trusty sidekick, Pando. Oh, thank you, Mr. Crowbar. Huh? Huh? Ooh, not half bad.
[00:03-00:05] Visual: A small, purple penguin character, wearing a red tie and a red scarf, sits at a wooden desk. A large, round, yellow seal with a leafy design is pinned to a blue curtain behind him. The desk has a sign that reads 'MR. PRESIDENT' in pink letters. The penguin looks directly at the camera with a neutral expression. | Text: MR. PRESIDENT
[00:29-00:56] Speech: Classic Pando. Oh, you guys are the best. It also makes me want to give you the keys to my Ferrari Turbo. Ah, Mr. President, we just couldn't. Wait a second. President would never give out his Ferrari Turbo to anyone. Who are you? Where's the real President? I knew it. A danger bot. What does this mean, Mr. Krollbar? It means that the President has been kidnapped.
[00:57-01:26] Speech: Are you guys good with popcorn and drinks? Do you need more pillows? Just holler at your boy if you need anything. Quick, man, can you make more popcorn? Looks like people are running low. Hey, what do you think? Dude, everyone is laughing at Pando. Cool, right? He's the comedy relief. Oh, look, there's a sale on rat traps. Meanwhile, back at the base. Who kidnapped the president? Could it have been Agent Cobra? Maybe it was my old nemesis, Neferio Dawson. It just doesn't make sense. Have you found anything, Pando?
[01:00-01:04] Visual: At night, a group of children, including a wolf, a boy with glasses, and a girl, sit on the ground with bored and sad expressions. The scene then cuts to the We Bare Bears' makeshift movie theater, where the Polar Bear is holding a bucket of popcorn and the Brown Bear looks on with his arms crossed.
[01:05-01:09] Visual: The scene cuts to the main characters, a panda and a bear, sitting with their parents. The panda, wearing a diaper, looks angry and crosses his arms. The bear looks sad and dejected. The scene then shows a hand-drawn map on a screen, detailing a 'cool base' with a watchtower, shark moat, and attack dog. | Text: cool base
[01:26-01:43] Speech: Not really. Security camera didn't pick up anything unusual. Wait a second. Let me see the computer. There. Enhance. Evil robot headquarters? This goes deeper than I thought. Come on, Pando. Let's head to the lab. Right behind you, Mr. Jones!
[01:52-02:02] Speech: Hello, Agent W. I'm stuck, Crowbar. Ah, Mr. Jones. Late as usual. Fashionably late, you mean? No, not really. All right, here you go.
[02:00-02:02] Visual: A close-up shot reveals a desk with a yellow backpack on it. The backpack has a white paper tag with 'JET' written on it. Next to it is a walking cane with a pair of glasses on top, and several bottles filled with colorful liquids and straws. On the wall behind, there's a drawing of a green bottle and a purple bottle, and a small window with a plant on the sill.
[02:03-02:08] Visual: A large brown bear with white hair, wearing a striped shirt and a blue bow tie, looks distressed. He holds the yellow 'JET' backpack, which is now on his back. He lifts a small blue dumbbell, his face contorting in anger and frustration as he grunts.
[02:04-02:28] Speech: Ended up doing some modifications to the jetpack. What's this weight for? That's to maintain your totally sweet ripped bod, my good man. Mm-hmm. Good thinking, W. I'll do a few thousand reps on my way there. Better come back in one piece, you hear? Of course. I promised my girlfriend an ice cream date. You're not coming back. I just know it. I hate you. I love you. I hate you. I love you. I hate you. I love you. I hate you. I love. I hate. I hate.
[02:30-02:59] Speech: Well, hey! Dude, I can hear you playing from over there. I'll give you your phone back after the movie. Why would I want to watch a movie that's constantly making fun of me? Just watch. Be supportive, man. No, you be supportive. Hey, look, man. It's the guy. Look at him. It's the guy. I don't know. Crowbar, help me! I think something's wrong with my jetpack! Get it together, Pando. Now let's dive!
[03:00-03:04] Visual: In an underwater scene, a small black bear character in a full-body wetsuit and snorkel gear floats in a fish tank. A large orange fish with a mechanical striped mouth swims past him. In the background, a skull with a sign reading 'EVIL ROBOT HQ' is visible, along with some seaweed and bubbles. | Text: EVIL ROBOT HQ
[03:02-03:25] Speech: Seems like you made a new friend, Pando. Ah! If only I knew how to swim! What? What? That's not true! I do know how to swim! We're here! Okay, Pando. I gotta find some place to freshen up. Oh, my phone got wet! Evil robot spa. Don't mind if I do.
[03:05-03:07] Visual: The scene cuts to a group of cartoon humans and animals sitting on blankets in a dark forest at night. They are all looking up with expressions of shock and concern. A large panda character stands behind them, gesturing with his hands as if explaining something.
[03:31-03:58] Speech: Ah, yeah. Check out those biceps. Yeah, you know. Crowbar Jones. Now do me a little tiny favor and break it down. Ah, what? What's going on? Oh, what is happening? Let's get out of here. Are people going to the bathroom? They're going to miss the best part. No, no, no, no, no. Please don't go. I promise it's going to get better.
[03:58-04:14] Speech: There's a cool explosion at the end. Wait, I swear this is a family film. I worked really hard on it. Okay, where's Grizz? He's gone too far this time.
[04:00-04:04] Visual: The scene opens on a dark, wooded clearing at night. Various cartoon animals, including a deer, a cat, and a polar bear in a red dress, are gathered. A large brown bear, Grizz, walks into the frame from the right, carrying a picnic basket. In the background, a man with a beard and a small child sit on the ground, watching a movie projected onto a large white screen.
[04:05-04:08] Visual: The camera cuts to a close-up of a white polar bear, Ice Bear, wearing a red dress and a small, pointed hat. He has a worried and slightly confused expression. Behind him, a panda, Bo, looks angry, with his arms crossed and a scowl on his face. A small grey wolf sits on a mat to the left.

It's okay, bro. I think I'm just gonna throw the movie in the trash anyways. Wait, really? The audience has spoken. Everyone hates my movie. All that hard work for nothing. It's all good, guys.
